T0332.title: OTC Exchange
T0332.definition: In the context of cybercrime, cashing out specifically refers to the operation of exchanging fiat currency for cryptocurrencies like USDT.
T0332.description: The cash-out merchant is a key link between the fiat currency pool and the cryptocurrency pool. They receive fiat funds from illegal activities such as fraud and gambling, then pay out an equivalent value in cryptocurrency to the counterparty. This process helps criminal enterprises 'clean' high-risk fiat currency into relatively anonymous virtual assets, moving funds from the regulated banking system into a gray zone, and represents the core exchange link in the money laundering chain.
T0332.usageExample: Accepting second-tier funds from Lingqiantong and Yu'ebao for cash-out back to USDT.

references.title: Exposing Fraud-Laundering Models: Order-Posting Recharge Laundering and Merchant Laundering Spread Rapidly
The Current Card Vendor Ecosystem Under the Card-Crackdown Campaign
